gene_region.label: TPSB2 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical TPSB2 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: TPSB2 encodes beta-2 tryptase, a mast-cell serine protease relevant to allergic inflammation. This topic bundle emphasizes allergy and inflammation, with interpretation centered on IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local TPSB2 bundle is oriented toward allergy and inflammation. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: TPSB2 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Allergy, asthma, atopy, and inflammatory disease context
Mast-cell, eosinophil, cytokine, and chemokine pathway biology
Immune signaling, barrier inflammation, and therapeutic target review
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: TPSB2 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for TPSB2.
Because TPSB2 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use TPSB2 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
Allergy, asthma, atopy, and inflammatory disease context
Mast-cell, eosinophil, cytokine, and chemokine pathway biology

biorender_visuals.label: TPSB2 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: TPSB2: allergy and inflammation via IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a TPSB2 allergy and inflammation thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_records.common_name: TPSB2 allergy and inflammation pathway model
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: TPSB2 immune-response variation model is curated as a TPSB2 marker for Allergy, asthma, atopy, and inflammatory disease context, Mast-cell, eosinophil, cytokine, and chemokine pathway biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
